hero_title:
Your agent doesn't need to
stop at Markdown.
hero_tagline: html-hoster receives HTML via API, CLI, or MCP and delivers a public URL ready to share.
hero_sub: No CMS. No dashboard. No build. No manual deploy.
hero_hook: Ask the AI to generate the page and publish it. Or send the HTML manually via the API.
how_title: Publish AI-generated HTML without manual deploy
how_sub: Send HTML, define the path, and get a public URL ready to share instantly.
how_step1_body: Choose your desired public path or slug (e.g. /my-page). No subdomains or complex setup required.
how_step2_body: Publish via API, CLI, or directly through your AI agent (via MCP) using your API key configured via environment variables.
how_step3_body: The server validates, stores (local or S3), and serves the static HTML immediately. No builds or dashboards.
chat_user: Create a page about astrophysics and publish it to /astro
chat_assistant_thinking: Understood. Generating a modern HTML design about astrophysics and publishing via MCP...
chat_assistant_done:
Done! The page is live:
🚀 html.yoursite.com/astro
feat_sub: Designed to be the publishing layer for any pipeline that generates HTML — manually or via AI.
feat1_body: All write routes require Authorization: Bearer. Key configured via environment variable.
feat2_body: Filesystem in development, S3-compatible (AWS, MinIO, R2) in production. Abstract layer switchable via STORAGE_DRIVER.
feat3_body: Built-in MCP server. Any compatible agent can see the tools to publish and manage pages directly.
feat4_body: Besides JSON, accepts .html file upload via multipart/form-data. Useful for pipelines with physical files.
feat5_body: Strict validation against path traversal, invalid segments, and reserved prefixes. No server-side logic from API-submitted HTML ever runs.
feat6_body: Swagger only visible in dev/demo. Demo mode disables writes and storage — great for showcasing the project risk-free.
feat7_body: OpenAPI 3.1 docs auto-generated at /api/docs. Spec available as JSON for integration with any tooling.
feat8_body: Fast runtime, native TypeScript, no build step needed in dev. Deploy anywhere that runs Bun.
api_sub: Simple HTTP interface. Any language, external tool, or AI with HTTP access can use it.
ep1_desc: Publish or update a page via JSON
mcp_title: Native MCP for any compatible agent
mcp_sub: Configure the MCP server once and allow your AI to publish, update, and manage HTML pages using structured tools.
mcp_box_sub: Your agent doesn't need to improvise HTTP calls. It receives typed tools to securely publish, list, query, and remove pages.
mcp_t1: Publish or update HTML at a path.
mcp_t2: Returns URL, metadata, and last-updated timestamp.
mcp_t4: Lists published pages and their URLs.
mcp_compatible_text: Compatible with: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, custom MCP clients, and other compatible agents.
mcp_config_desc: The example below demonstrates integration with Claude Desktop. The server works identically in any compatible client.
cta_title: Ready to publish your first page?
cta_sub: Open source, MIT, no license cost. Deploy anywhere that runs Bun.
